---
# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-051
# Cross-Property Player Recognition and Loyalty Entitlement
# Generated by build-pipeline (lead-architect scaffold + 5 expert consultations)

prd: PRD-051
prd_title: "Cross-Property Player Recognition and Loyalty Entitlement"
service: CasinoService (primary), LoyaltyService, PlayerService, VisitService
mvp_phase: 2
adr_refs: [ADR-044, ADR-043, ADR-024, ADR-030, ADR-018, ADR-015]
gov010_check: "waived:EXEC-051 implements the already-approved ADR-044 architectural pattern (cross-context recognition, RLS widening boundary, scalar extraction model, loyalty mechanics) rather than introducing a new cross-context access model, tenancy model, or loyalty mechanics variant. No new ADR escalation required."
slice_model: true  # Slice 1 ships independently; Slice 2 blocked on player-exclusion merge

workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: "Dual-Mode RLS Policies + Recognition RPCs"
    description: >
      2 dual-mode SELECT policies (player_casino, player_loyalty) per ADR-044 D2.
      3 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (lookup, activate, redeem) per ADR-044 D3/D4/D6.
      Audit event types. Exclusion fields stubbed (null) for Slice 1.
    executor: rls-expert
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    slice: 1
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/20260313190519_prd051_company_scoped_recognition.sql
    patterns:
      - "ADR-044 D2: Dual-mode SELECT (casino path + company path, fail-closed)"
      - "ADR-024 INV-8: No client-supplied casino_id/actor_id"
      - "ADR-018 Template 5: SECURITY DEFINER context injection"
      - "ADR-030 D4: Write-path session vars only on critical tables (loyalty_ledger)"
      - "ADR-015 Pattern C: Hybrid context with JWT fallback (SELECT)"
    implementation_notes:
      - "SECURITY DEFINER RPCs bypass RLS — implement company-scoping in SQL WHERE clauses, not via RLS policies. Tier 1 dual-mode RLS policies cover direct table access outside RPCs."
      - "full_name is computed: first_name || ' ' || last_name AS full_name (player table has no full_name column)"
      - "Search pattern: (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) ILIKE '%' || p_search_term || '%' — leverages existing trigram index"
      - "Use CTE-based query joining player → player_casino → player_loyalty, with lateral join for last_company_visit. Avoid row-by-row loops to prevent N+1."
      - "Verify casino.company_id index exists (ADR-043). If missing, add: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_casino_company_id ON casino(company_id)"
      - "Activation RPC must also create player_loyalty row: INSERT INTO player_loyalty (player_id, casino_id, balance, tier) VALUES (p_player_id, v_casino_id, 0, NULL) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — mirrors rpc_create_player pattern. WS1 must verify that player_loyalty has a unique constraint on (player_id, casino_id) before relying on ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotent activation."
      - "Benchmarking must verify the actual query plan for the chosen search expression. If the concatenated-name search does not use an acceptable index path, add an expression trigram index on (first_name || ' ' || last_name) or revise the search implementation before claiming the <200ms p95 target is satisfied."
      - "Role-gating: all 3 recognition RPCs are restricted to pit_boss and admin (Option A — PRD-051 must be updated to explicitly state this broader rule for consistency)"
    validation:
      - "npm run db:types-local exits 0"
      - "Existing player_casino/player_loyalty tests still pass"
      - "Policy allowlist CI gate: only player_casino and player_loyalty SELECT reference app.company_id"
      - "casino.company_id index exists"
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS2:
    name: "Service Layer — Recognition + Entitlement"
    description: >
      New services/recognition/ namespace with DTOs, mappers, keys, and RPC wrappers.
      Thin functional factory wrapping the 3 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    slice: 1
    outputs:
      - services/recognition/dtos.ts
      - services/recognition/mappers.ts
      - services/recognition/keys.ts
      - services/recognition/crud.ts
      - services/recognition/http.ts
      - services/recognition/schemas.ts
      - services/recognition/index.ts
      - docs/70-governance/METRIC_PROVENANCE_MATRIX.md  # MEAS-013 amendment (portfolio_total)
    patterns:
      - "Pattern A (Contract-First): Manual DTOs for composite RPC results"
      - "Functional factory: createRecognitionService(supabase)"
      - "Explicit interface: RecognitionServiceInterface"
      - "React Query key factory with .scope for surgical invalidation"
    validation:
      - "npm run type-check exits 0"
      - "No as any, no ReturnType<>"
      - "DTOs derive from Database types where possible, manual for composite"
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3:
    name: "Route Handlers — Recognition API"
    description: >
      3 API endpoints: company player lookup (POST), local activation (POST),
      local redemption (POST). ServiceHttpResult contract, Zod validation.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    slice: 1
    outputs:
      - app/api/v1/players/lookup-company/route.ts
      - app/api/v1/players/activate-locally/route.ts
      - app/api/v1/players/redeem-loyalty/route.ts
      - docs/25-api-data/API_SURFACE_MVP.md  # 3 new routes added
      - docs/25-api-data/api-surface.openapi.yaml  # OpenAPI spec update
    patterns:
      - "withServerAction middleware for auth/RLS/audit"
      - "Zod input schemas from services/recognition/schemas.ts"
      - "requireIdempotencyKey for activation + redemption (mutations)"
      - "ServiceHttpResult<T> response envelope"
      - "ADR-041 Surface Classification: BFF RPC Aggregation (lookup)"
    validation:
      - "npm run type-check exits 0"
      - "npm run lint exits 0"
      - "All routes use withServerAction wrapper"
      - "Idempotency enforced on mutations"
    gate: lint
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS4:
    name: "Frontend — Company-Aware Recognition UI"
    description: >
      Extend player search with company-aware recognition cards (3 states: A, B1/B2, C).
      Loyalty entitlement display, activation flow, redemption flow.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS3]
    slice: 1
    outputs:
      - components/player-dashboard/recognition-card.tsx
      - components/player-dashboard/loyalty-entitlement-display.tsx
      - components/player-dashboard/activate-locally-dialog.tsx
      - components/player-dashboard/redeem-loyalty-dialog.tsx
      - hooks/recognition/use-recognition.ts
      - hooks/recognition/use-recognition-mutations.ts
    patterns:
      - "React 19: useTransition for all async mutations (activation, redemption)"
      - "TanStack Query v5: useQuery for lookup, useMutation for writes"
      - "Optimistic updates with rollback on mutation failure"
      - "Key-based reset for dialog state (no useEffect sync)"
      - "ADR-041 Surface Classification required (4 mandatory fields)"
    surface_classification:
      rendering_delivery: "Client Shell — interactive search with debounced input, client-rendered result cards"
      data_aggregation: "BFF RPC Aggregation — rpc_lookup_player_company collapses 4 bounded contexts into single call (GOV-PAT-003)"
      rejected_patterns:
        - "RSC Prefetch rejected: search is interactive/user-initiated, not page-load data (§4 Q1)"
        - "Simple Query rejected: recognition requires cross-context JOIN across Player, Casino, Loyalty, Visit (§4 Q2)"
      metric_provenance:
        - "portfolio_total: SUM(player_loyalty.balance) — new metric, requires MEAS-013 registration"
        - "local_balance: player_loyalty.balance — MEAS-006 (Loyalty Points Balance, Raw Record, per-request fresh)"
        - "redeemable_here: equals local_balance under D6 debit model — derived from MEAS-006"
    validation:
      - "npm run type-check exits 0"
      - "npm run lint exits 0"
      - "npm run build exits 0"
      - "No useEffect sync anti-pattern"
      - "All mutations use useTransition"
      - "portfolio_total clearly labeled as awareness (never 'Available balance')"
    gate: build
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS5:
    name: "Integration Tests — Cross-Company Isolation + Redemption"
    description: >
      RPC contract tests, cross-company isolation, single-casino regression,
      concurrent redemption atomicity, audit event verification, CI gate scripts.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1, WS2]
    slice: 1
    outputs:
      - services/recognition/__tests__/rpc-contract.int.test.ts
      - services/recognition/__tests__/mappers.test.ts
      - services/recognition/__tests__/http-contract.test.ts
      - scripts/ci/check-company-policy-allowlist.sh
    patterns:
      - "Multi-company test fixtures: 2 companies, 3 casinos, cross-enrolled players"
      - "RPC contract testing: authenticated client with set_rls_context_from_staff()"
      - "Concurrent redemption: Promise.all with same player, verify no negative balance"
      - "Negative testing: cross-company access denied, unauthenticated rejected"
      - "Role enforcement: dealer → FORBIDDEN, cashier → FORBIDDEN on all 3 RPCs"
    validation:
      - "All RPC contract tests pass"
      - "Cross-company isolation verified (Company X staff sees zero Company Y data)"
      - "Single-casino regression: existing player/loyalty tests unchanged"
      - "Concurrent redemption: no negative balances"
      - "CI gate script: policy allowlist check exits 0"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS6:
    name: "Exclusion Safety Signal — RPC Enhancement"
    description: >
      Slice 2: Populate exclusion fields in recognition RPC. Add D5 severity
      enforcement to activation and redemption RPCs. Requires player-exclusion merge.
    executor: rls-expert
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    external_blocker: "player-exclusion branch merge into feat/dual-bondary-tenancy"
    slice: 2
    blocked: true
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_prd051_exclusion_safety_signal.sql
    patterns:
      - "ADR-044 D5: Severity-based exclusion policy (hard_block/soft_alert/monitor)"
      - "Tier 2 scalar extraction from player_exclusion (SECURITY DEFINER, no RLS change)"
      - "FR-8: soft_alert override audit with actor, player, casino, reason, timestamp"
    validation:
      - "hard_block blocks activation + redemption server-side"
      - "soft_alert requires admin override; override audited"
      - "monitor produces warning only"
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS7:
    name: "Exclusion UI — Safety Warnings + Override Flow"
    description: >
      Slice 2: B2 sub-state warning banners, severity-based activation blocking,
      admin override flow with audit reason capture.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS6, WS4]
    slice: 2
    blocked: true
    outputs:
      - components/player-dashboard/exclusion-warning-banner.tsx
      - components/player-dashboard/admin-override-dialog.tsx
    patterns:
      - "Severity-based UX: hard_block (disabled), soft_alert (override dialog), monitor (info badge)"
      - "Admin override: captures reason text, audited via FR-8"
    validation:
      - "hard_block disables activation/redemption buttons"
      - "soft_alert shows override dialog with reason field"
      - "monitor shows informational badge only"
    gate: build
    estimated_complexity: medium

execution_phases:
  - name: "Phase 1 — Database Foundation"
    parallel: [WS1]
    gates: [schema-validation]
    description: "RLS policies + RPCs. Type generation gate."

  - name: "Phase 2 — Service Layer"
    parallel: [WS2]
    gates: [type-check]
    description: "Recognition service factory, DTOs, keys."

  - name: "Phase 3 — API + Tests"
    parallel: [WS3, WS5]
    gates: [lint, test-pass]
    description: "Route handlers and integration tests in parallel."

  - name: "Phase 4 — Frontend"
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [build]
    description: "Company-aware recognition UI."

  - name: "Phase 5 — Exclusion Safety Signal (Slice 2)"
    parallel: [WS6, WS7]
    gates: [schema-validation, build]
    blocked: true
    blocker: "player-exclusion branch merge into feat/dual-bondary-tenancy"
    description: "Exclusion signal population + severity-based UX."

gates:
  schema-validation:
    command: "npm run db:types-local"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, types regenerated"

  type-check:
    command: "npm run type-check"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no type errors"

  lint:
    command: "npm run lint --quiet"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no errors"

  test-pass:
    command: "npm test -- --testPathPattern='services/recognition' --silent"
    success_criteria: "All tests pass"

  build:
    command: "npm run build"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no build errors"

external_dependencies:
  - prd: ADR-043
    service: CasinoService
    status: implemented
    required_for: "company table, app.company_id session variable"

  - prd: ADR-042
    service: PlayerExclusionService
    status: "on player-exclusion branch (not merged)"
    required_for: "Slice 2 — exclusion safety signal"

adversarial_review:
  verdict: "Ship w/ gates"
  attempt: 1
  reviewers:
    R1_security_tenancy: "Ship w/ gates (0 P0, 2 P1)"
    R2_architecture_boundaries: "Ship w/ gates (0 P0, 1 P1)"
    R3_implementation_completeness: "Ship w/ gates (0 P0, 5 P1)"
    R4_test_quality: "Ship w/ gates (0 P0, 3 P1)"
    R5_performance_operability: "Ship w/ gates (0 P0, 2 P1)"
  consolidated: "0 P0, 9 P1 (deduplicated). All P1s patched inline."
  patch_applied: true
  audit_delta_applied: true  # 7 corrections from exec-051-audit-delta.md

branch_note: "feat/dual-bondary-tenancy is the canonical branch name (typo 'bondary' baked into git at creation). Renaming mid-development would disrupt worktree. All spec references use the actual branch name."

risks:
  - risk: "player-exclusion branch not merged blocks Slice 2"
    severity: P1
    mitigation: "Slice 1 ships independently. Exclusion fields stubbed as null. Safety gap documented."

  - risk: "Cross-company data leakage"
    severity: P0
    mitigation: "Fail-closed policy (Path 2 requires non-null app.company_id) + CI allowlist gate + shadow testing"

  - risk: "Company-scoped EXISTS subquery performance"
    severity: P2
    mitigation: "casino.company_id is indexed. Benchmark during WS1. <200ms p95 SLO."

  - risk: "Stale entitlement data"
    severity: P2
    mitigation: "Per-request reads, no cross-request caching."

  - risk: "ADR-044 references current_balance but actual column is balance"
    severity: P1
    mitigation: "RPCs use actual column name (balance). Document errata in implementation."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-051 — Cross-Property Player Recognition and Loyalty Entitlement

## Overview

Phase 2 of Dual-Boundary Tenancy. Staff at their home casino can look up players enrolled at sister properties under the same company, view the player's portfolio loyalty entitlement, receive exclusion safety signals, activate the player locally, and redeem allowed entitlement through local workflows. Staff never switches casino context.

**Two-Slice Delivery Model:**
- **Slice 1** (WS1–WS5): Core recognition + entitlement — shippable independently
- **Slice 2** (WS6–WS7): Exclusion safety signal — blocked on `player-exclusion` branch merge

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Company-scoped player lookup (name search across sister properties)
- Loyalty entitlement visibility (portfolio total + local balance + redeemable_here)
- Local activation (create player_casino row at current property)
- Local redemption (debit local balance atomically)
- Audit trail (company_lookup, local_activation, loyalty_redemption)
- Exclusion safety signals (Slice 2 — has_sister_exclusions, max_exclusion_severity)

**Out of Scope:**
- Staff switching casino context
- Raw cross-property visit/financial/compliance data exposure
- Cross-property balance pooling
- ADR-024 INV-8 amendment
- player_loyalty.preferences cross-property exposure

## Architecture Context

- **ADR-044** (Accepted): All 7 decisions frozen (D1–D7)
- **ADR-043** (Implemented): Phase 1 foundation — company table, app.company_id
- **ADR-024** (Frozen): Authoritative context derivation — INV-8 intact
- **ADR-030** (Accepted): Auth pipeline hardening — write-path session vars only
- **SRM v4.18.0**: CasinoService owns player_casino; LoyaltyService owns player_loyalty, loyalty_ledger

**Schema Note:** `player_loyalty.balance` is the actual column name. ADR-044 D4/D6 reference `current_balance` — the RPCs use the actual column name `balance`.

## Workstream Details

### WS1: Dual-Mode RLS Policies + Recognition RPCs

**Purpose:** Create the database foundation — company-scoped read policies and 3 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs.

**Migration:** `supabase/migrations/20260313190519_prd051_company_scoped_recognition.sql`

**RLS Policy Changes (2 tables):**

1. **player_casino** — DROP existing `player_casino_select_same_casino`, CREATE `player_casino_select_company` with dual-mode pattern (ADR-044 D2)
2. **player_loyalty** — DROP existing `player_loyalty_select`, CREATE `player_loyalty_select_company` with dual-mode pattern (ADR-044 D2)

Write policies (INSERT/UPDATE) on both tables remain UNCHANGED (casino-scoped).

**RPCs (3 SECURITY DEFINER functions):**

1. **`rpc_lookup_player_company(p_search_term text)`** — D4
   - SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path TO 'public'
   - Calls `set_rls_context_from_staff()` first
   - Searches `player` by `(first_name || ' ' || last_name) ILIKE '%' || p_search_term || '%'` (existing trigram index)
   - Returns `first_name || ' ' || last_name AS full_name` (computed — player table has no full_name column)
   - Reads `player_casino` with company-scoped WHERE clause (SECURITY DEFINER bypasses RLS — must implement company-scoping in SQL)
   - Reads `player_loyalty` with company-scoped WHERE clause — projects `balance` + `tier` only
   - Uses CTE-based query with lateral join to avoid N+1 (joins player → player_casino → player_loyalty → visit)
   - Reads `visit` inside SECURITY DEFINER — returns `MAX(started_at)` scalar only (Tier 2)
   - Exclusion fields: returns `NULL::boolean` / `NULL::text` in Slice 1
   - Computes `loyalty_entitlement` JSON per D7
   - Computes `active_locally` by checking if player_casino row exists at caller's casino
   - Logs `company_lookup` audit event
   - Role gate: `app.staff_role IN ('pit_boss', 'admin')`
   - REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC, anon; GRANT TO authenticated

2. **`rpc_activate_player_locally(p_player_id uuid)`** — D3
   - SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path TO 'public'
   - Calls `set_rls_context_from_staff()` first
   - Validates player exists within caller's company boundary (EXISTS on player_casino + casino.company_id)
   - Exclusion check: no-op in Slice 1 (WS6 adds D5 enforcement)
   - Creates `player_casino` row at caller's `app.casino_id` (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — idempotent)
   - Creates `player_loyalty` row at caller's casino (balance=0, tier=NULL, INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) — mirrors `rpc_create_player` atomic pattern
   - Logs `local_activation` audit event with actor, player, casino, timestamp
   - Returns activation result
   - Role gate: `app.staff_role IN ('pit_boss', 'admin')`

3. **`rpc_redeem_loyalty_locally(p_player_id uuid, p_amount integer, p_reason text)`** — D6
   - SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path TO 'public'
   - Calls `set_rls_context_from_staff()` first
   - Validates player is enrolled at caller's casino
   - Exclusion check: no-op in Slice 1
   - Atomic debit: `UPDATE player_loyalty SET balance = balance - p_amount WHERE casino_id = v_casino_id AND player_id = p_player_id AND balance >= p_amount`
   - If UPDATE affects 0 rows → RAISE EXCEPTION 'INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE'
   - Creates local `loyalty_ledger` entry at caller's casino (casino-scoped write)
   - Logs `loyalty_redemption` audit event
   - Returns updated entitlement state (local + portfolio total)
   - Role gate: `app.staff_role IN ('pit_boss', 'admin')`
   - `loyalty_ledger` is a critical table → session-var-only write (ADR-030 D4)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `npm run db:types-local` succeeds
- [ ] Existing player_casino and player_loyalty tests still pass
- [ ] Only `player_casino` and `player_loyalty` SELECT policies reference `app.company_id`
- [ ] No other table's RLS references `app.company_id`
- [ ] All 3 RPCs call `set_rls_context_from_staff()` as first statement
- [ ] No RPC accepts `p_casino_id` or `p_actor_id` (ADR-024 INV-8)
- [ ] Migration ends with `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema'`

---

### WS2: Service Layer — Recognition + Entitlement

**Purpose:** New `services/recognition/` namespace with typed RPC wrappers.

**Decision: New Namespace (`services/recognition/`)**
- Justified: cross-cutting concern spanning 4 bounded contexts (Player, Casino, Loyalty, Visit)
- Not extending CasinoService: recognition is a distinct capability, not casino CRUD
- Not extending PlayerService: recognition returns composite data beyond player identity
- Over-Engineering Guardrail: passes — 3 RPCs, distinct DTOs, clear bounded concern

**Service Interface:**

```typescript
export interface RecognitionServiceInterface {
  lookupCompany(searchTerm: string): Promise<RecognitionResultDTO[]>;
  activateLocally(playerId: string): Promise<ActivationResultDTO>;
  redeemLocally(playerId: string, amount: number, reason: string): Promise<RedemptionResultDTO>;
}
```

**DTOs (Pattern A — manual, composite RPC results):**

- `RecognitionResultDTO` — maps from rpc_lookup_player_company result
- `LoyaltyEntitlementDTO` — typed JSON shape from D7
- `EnrolledCasinoDTO` — typed enrollment badge
- `ActivationResultDTO` — activation success/noop + exclusion status
- `RedemptionResultDTO` — updated entitlement state after redemption

**React Query Key Factory:**
```typescript
export const recognitionKeys = {
  all: ['recognition'] as const,
  lookup: (searchTerm: string) => [...recognitionKeys.all, 'lookup', searchTerm] as const,
  // Mutations: activate, redeem (no query keys)
}
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Functional factory pattern (no classes)
- [ ] Explicit interface (no ReturnType<>)
- [ ] DTOs in dtos.ts, mappers in mappers.ts
- [ ] No `as any` casts
- [ ] Types from `@/types/database.types`
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0

---

### WS3: Route Handlers — Recognition API

**Purpose:** 3 API endpoints exposing recognition RPCs.

**Routes:**

| Method | Path | Purpose | Idempotency |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/v1/players/lookup-company` | Company player search | Not required (read) |
| POST | `/api/v1/players/activate-locally` | Local activation | Required (mutation) |
| POST | `/api/v1/players/redeem-loyalty` | Local redemption | Required (mutation) |

**Why POST for lookup:** The search term is a request body parameter, not a URL query. POST avoids URL-encoding issues with names and is consistent with RPC-style invocation.

**Zod Schemas (in `services/recognition/schemas.ts`):**
- `LookupCompanyInput` — `{ search_term: z.string().min(2).max(100) }`
- `ActivateLocallyInput` — `{ player_id: z.string().uuid() }`
- `RedeemLoyaltyInput` — `{ player_id: z.string().uuid(), amount: z.number().int().positive(), reason: z.string().min(1).max(500) }`

**Error Mapping:**
- `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` → 422 Unprocessable Entity
- `UNAUTHORIZED` → 401
- `FORBIDDEN` (role check) → 403
- `NOT_FOUND` (player not in company) → 404
- `VALIDATION_ERROR` (Zod) → 400

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All routes use `withServerAction` wrapper
- [ ] Zod validation on all inputs
- [ ] `requireIdempotencyKey` on activation + redemption
- [ ] Response uses `ServiceHttpResult<T>`
- [ ] `npm run lint` exits 0

---

### WS4: Frontend — Company-Aware Recognition UI

**Purpose:** Company-aware player recognition with 3-state UX, loyalty entitlement, activation, and redemption.

**Component Hierarchy:**
```
components/player-dashboard/
├── recognition-card.tsx            # State A/B1/B2/C card with badges
├── loyalty-entitlement-display.tsx # Portfolio total vs redeemable_here
├── activate-locally-dialog.tsx     # Confirmation dialog for local activation
└── redeem-loyalty-dialog.tsx       # Redemption form with amount/reason

hooks/recognition/
├── use-recognition.ts              # useQuery wrapper for lookup
└── use-recognition-mutations.ts    # useMutation for activate + redeem
```

**Integration Point:** Extends existing `PlayerSearchCommand` component. When company_id exists and search returns cross-property results, the result list renders `RecognitionCard` instead of the standard player entry.

**Surface Classification (ADR-041):**
- **Rendering Delivery:** Client Shell — interactive search with debounced input
- **Data Aggregation:** BFF RPC Aggregation (GOV-PAT-003) — `rpc_lookup_player_company` collapses 4 bounded contexts
- **Rejected:** RSC Prefetch (search is user-initiated, not page-load); Simple Query (cross-context JOIN)
- **Metric Provenance:** `local_balance` maps to MEAS-006; `portfolio_total` requires MEAS-013 registration

**Key UX Rules:**
- `portfolio_total` label: "Portfolio total (all properties)" — NEVER "Available balance"
- `redeemable_here` label: "Available here" — the only actionable number
- Redemption dialog shows ONLY `redeemable_here`, not portfolio total
- Single-casino staff (companies with 1 casino) see no behavioral change

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Three-state rendering (A, B1/B2, C)
- [ ] All mutations use `useTransition`
- [ ] Optimistic updates with rollback
- [ ] No useEffect sync anti-pattern
- [ ] ADR-041 Surface Classification present
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0

---

### WS5: Integration Tests — Cross-Company Isolation + Redemption

**Purpose:** Verify all DoD gates with automated tests.

**Test Files:**
- `services/recognition/__tests__/rpc-contract.int.test.ts` — RPC-level integration tests
- `services/recognition/__tests__/mappers.test.ts` — Mapper unit tests (100% coverage)
- `services/recognition/__tests__/http-contract.test.ts` — Route handler contract tests
- `scripts/ci/check-company-policy-allowlist.sh` — CI gate for policy allowlist

**Test Fixture Design:**
```
Company Alpha (company_alpha_id)
├── Casino A (casino_a_id) — staff_a (pit_boss)
└── Casino B (casino_b_id) — staff_b (pit_boss), staff_b_admin (admin)

Company Beta (company_beta_id)
└── Casino C (casino_c_id) — staff_c (pit_boss)

Players:
├── player_1 — enrolled at Casino A only, loyalty balance: 8000
├── player_2 — enrolled at Casino A + Casino B, loyalty: A=8000, B=4500
├── player_3 — enrolled at Casino C only (Company Beta)
```

**Test Categories:**

1. **Cross-Company Isolation (P0)**
   - staff_b lookups player_3 (Company Beta) → empty results
   - staff_c lookups player_1 (Company Alpha) → empty results
   - staff_b activates player from Company Beta → FORBIDDEN

2. **Company-Scoped Recognition**
   - staff_b lookups "player_1" → finds player_1 at Casino A, active_locally=false
   - staff_a lookups "player_2" → finds player_2 at both casinos, active_locally=true
   - Loyalty entitlement: portfolio_total=12500 (8000+4500), local_balance=4500

3. **Local Activation**
   - staff_b activates player_1 at Casino B → player_casino row created
   - staff_b re-activates player_1 → idempotent (no error, no duplicate row)
   - Audit event logged with correct actor, player, casino

4. **Local Redemption**
   - staff_b redeems 2000 from player_2 at Casino B → balance: 4500→2500
   - staff_b redeems 5000 from player_2 → INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE
   - Audit event logged with amount, reason

5. **Concurrent Redemption**
   - 10 parallel redemptions of 500 from balance of 4500
   - Verify: exactly 9 succeed, 1 fails, final balance = 0

6. **Role Enforcement**
   - dealer role calls rpc_lookup_player_company → FORBIDDEN
   - cashier role calls rpc_activate_player_locally → FORBIDDEN
   - dealer/cashier calls rpc_redeem_loyalty_locally → FORBIDDEN
   - unauthenticated client calls any RPC → UNAUTHORIZED

7. **Lookup Contract — Ambiguous Input**
   - Multiple partial matches → returns all matching players (deterministic ordering by last_name, first_name)
   - Duplicate-name collisions → each result distinguishable by player_id + enrollment badges
   - Result cap → maximum 50 results per search (or configurable limit)
   - Minimum search length → p_search_term < 2 chars → VALIDATION_ERROR (enforced by Zod min(2) + RPC guard)
   - Empty result → returns empty array, no error
   - Single-character search → rejected at Zod layer (400)

8. **Single-Casino Regression**
   - Company with single casino → existing behavior unchanged
   - Existing player search tests pass without modification

9. **Policy Allowlist CI Gate**
   - `check-company-policy-allowlist.sh` scans all migrations
   - Fails if any table besides player_casino/player_loyalty references `app.company_id` in RLS

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All RPC contract tests pass
- [ ] Cross-company isolation verified
- [ ] Concurrent redemption: no negative balances
- [ ] Policy allowlist CI gate passes
- [ ] Mapper tests: 100% coverage

---

### WS6: Exclusion Safety Signal — RPC Enhancement (Slice 2, BLOCKED)

**Purpose:** Populate exclusion fields in recognition RPC, add D5 severity enforcement.

**Blocked on:** `player-exclusion` branch merge into `feat/dual-bondary-tenancy`

**Changes:**
- Enhance `rpc_lookup_player_company`: query `player_exclusion` inside SECURITY DEFINER, return `has_sister_exclusions` (boolean) and `max_exclusion_severity` (text)
- Enhance `rpc_activate_player_locally`: enforce D5 severity policy before activation
- Enhance `rpc_redeem_loyalty_locally`: enforce D5 severity policy before redemption
- Add `soft_alert` override audit (FR-8): actor, player, casino, override_reason, timestamp
- Add `p_override_reason text DEFAULT NULL` parameter to activation/redemption RPCs for admin overrides
- **Override reason fail-closed**: For soft_alert override flows, override reason is mandatory at UI (non-empty input required), route validation (Zod `z.string().min(1)`), and RPC layers (RAISE EXCEPTION if p_override_reason is null/blank when soft_alert override is invoked). No activation/redemption mutation may proceed without a valid override reason.

---

### WS7: Exclusion UI — Safety Warnings + Override Flow (Slice 2, BLOCKED)

**Purpose:** Severity-based warning UX for B2 sub-state.

**Blocked on:** WS6 completion

**Components:**
- `exclusion-warning-banner.tsx` — severity-adaptive banner (hard_block: red/disabled, soft_alert: amber/override, monitor: blue/info)
- `admin-override-dialog.tsx` — captures override reason, requires admin role

---

## Definition of Done

### Slice 1 (shippable independently)

- [ ] `rpc_lookup_player_company` returns correct results scoped to caller's company
- [ ] `rpc_activate_player_locally` creates enrollment with idempotency and audit trail
- [ ] `rpc_redeem_loyalty_locally` debits local balance atomically with balance guard
- [ ] Company-scoped SELECT policies exist only on `player_casino` and `player_loyalty` (CI gate)
- [ ] No RLS policy on `visit`, `player_exclusion`, or any other table references `app.company_id` (CI gate)
- [ ] Cross-company isolation verified (Company X staff sees zero Company Y data)
- [ ] Single-casino staff experience unchanged (existing test suite passes)
- [ ] Recognition search latency <200ms p95
- [ ] Concurrent redemption does not produce negative balances
- [ ] Audit events logged for lookup, activation, and redemption
- [ ] All gates pass (type-check, lint, test, build)
- [ ] No `as any`, no `console.*` in production code
- [ ] ADR-024 INV-8 intact (no client-supplied casino_id)
- [ ] ADR-030 compliance: loyalty_ledger writes use session vars only

### Slice 2 (ships after player-exclusion merge)

- [ ] Recognition RPC includes `has_sister_exclusions` and `max_exclusion_severity`
- [ ] `hard_block` severity blocks activation and redemption server-side
- [ ] `soft_alert` severity requires `admin` role override; override decision audited (FR-8)
- [ ] `monitor` severity produces warning only; no workflow blocking
- [ ] Integration test: exclusion at Casino A → recognition from Casino B returns correct signal

## SRM Update (v4.19.0 — upon implementation)

**CasinoService additions:**
- New RPCs: `rpc_lookup_player_company`, `rpc_activate_player_locally`
- Cross-context consumption: reads PlayerService (player), LoyaltyService (player_loyalty), VisitService (visit)

**LoyaltyService additions:**
- New RPC: `rpc_redeem_loyalty_locally`
- Cross-context audit: writes to CasinoService audit_log

**New bounded context registration:**
- RecognitionService (services/recognition/) — cross-cutting read model + activation/redemption wrappers
